title: ulmus cornubiensis
intro: Ulmus cornubiensis, also known as the Cornish elm, is a species of elm tree native to the southwestern peninsula of England, particularly in Cornwall. The plant's bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Ulmus cornubiensis include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of the Cornish elm often include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are prepared by steeping the bark and leaves in hot water or a solvent to create a medicinal extract.
